title: pothos englerianus
intro: Pothos englerianus, also known as the Engler's pothos or ivy arum, is a type of flowering plant in the family Arum. It is a climbing vine with heart-shaped leaves and is native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands and Southeast Asia. The medicinal properties of Pothos englerianus include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever and skin infections. Herbal preparations of Pothos englerianus, such as infusions and poultices, have been traditionally used to promote wound healing, reduce swelling, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
